title: Outdated TLS protocol version enabled
description: Server configuration allows TLS 1.0 or TLS 1.1 connections, which have known vulnerabilities (BEAST, POODLE). Only TLS 1.2+ should be accepted.

remediation.description: Configure the server to only accept TLS 1.2 and TLS 1.3. Disable SSLv3, TLS 1.0, and TLS 1.1.

title: Weak cipher suite enabled
description: Server configuration includes weak cipher suites (RC4, DES, 3DES, NULL, export-grade) that are vulnerable to known attacks.

remediation.description: Use only strong cipher suites with forward secrecy. Follow Mozilla's recommended configurations.

title: Missing or weak HSTS configuration
description: HTTP Strict Transport Security header is missing or has a max-age below the recommended 1 year (31536000 seconds), leaving users vulnerable to SSL stripping attacks.

remediation.description: Set the HSTS header with a max-age of at least 1 year and includeSubDomains.

title: Self-signed or invalid certificate in production
description: Application uses a self-signed certificate or disables certificate verification, making it vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks.

remediation.description: Use valid CA-signed certificates in production. Never disable certificate verification outside of development.
remediation.code_example:
// Remove NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0 and use proper CA certificates
const agent = new https.Agent({ ca: fs.readFileSync('ca-cert.pem') });

title: Missing certificate pinning for mobile/critical applications
description: Application connecting to backend APIs does not implement certificate pinning, making it susceptible to man-in-the-middle attacks with rogue CA certificates.

remediation.description: Implement certificate pinning for connections to your own backend services. Use public key pinning rather than certificate pinning for easier rotation.
